sections.paragraph:
ἅπαξ adv. (Hom.+; Tat. 6, 1 [Hb 9:26?]) ‘once’.

① as a numer. term pert. to a single occurrence, once, ἅ. ἐλιθάσθην I was stoned once 2 Cor 11:25. ἅ. πεφανέρωται Hb 9:26. ἅ. ἀποθανεῖν vs. 27 (Proverbia Aesopi 141 P.: ‹πλέον ἢ› ἅπαξ οὐδεὶς ἄνθρωπος θνῄσκει); cp. 1 Pt 3:18. ἅ. προσενεχθείς Hb 9:28. W. gen. foll. ἅ. τοῦ ἐνιαυτοῦ (Hdt. 2, 59; Ex 30:10; Lev 16:34) once a year Hb 9:7. ἔτι ἅ. (2 Macc 3:37; Judg 16:18, 28; TestAbr A 85, 15 [Stone p. 18] al.) once more=for the last time (Aeschyl., Ag. 1322; Judg 6:39) 12:26f (Hg 2:6). ἅ. καὶ δίς (Dionys. Hal. 8, 56, 1 οὐχ ἅ. ἀλλὰ καὶ δίς; Ael. Aristid. 36, 91 K.=48 p. 474 D.: ἅ. ἢ δίς; Anna Comn., Alexias 3, 3 ed. Reiff. I 102, 17 καὶ ἅ. καὶ δίς; 1 Km 17:39; 2 Esdr 23:20; 1 Macc 3:30) again and again, more than once (LMorris, NovT 1, ’56, 205–8) Phil 4:16; 1 Th 2:18; 1 Cl 53:3 (Dt 9:13). W. weakening of the numer. idea ἐπεὶ ἅ. (Thu. 7, 44, 7; X., An. 1, 9, 10; Menand., PDidot 36 S. p. 329; Menand., Dyscolos 392; Chion, Ep. 14, 1; POxy 1102, 8 ἐπεὶ ἄ. προσῆλθε τῇ κληρονομίᾳ) since for once Hv 3, 3, 4; m 4, 4, 1.

② pert. to a single occurrence and decisively unique, once and for all (Hippocr., Ep. 27, 41; Aelian, VH 2, 30; Philostrat., Ep. 7, 2; PLips 34, 20; 35, 19; Ps 88:36; PsSol 12:6; TestAbr 20 p. 103, 1 [Stone p. 54]; Philo, Ebr. 198; Jos., Bell. 2, 158, Ant. 4, 140; Just., A I, 61, 5) Hb 10:2; Jd 3, 5.—Sim. once (Alciphron 1, 8, 4; 1, 10, 2) Hb 6:4.—DELG s.v. πάξ and πήγνυμι. EDNT. M-M. TW. Spicq.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

gen. gen. = genitive

foll. foll. = followed, following

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

NovT NovT = Novum Testamentum (periodical)—List 6

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

PDidot PDidot , cited in FSandbach, Menandri Reliquiae Selectae; s. Menand.—List 5

Chion Chion , IV b.c.; for letters attributed to him, I a.d., s. Ep.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Hv Hv = Visions

m m = Mandates

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

PLips PLips = Griech. Urkunden der Papyrussammlung zu Leipzig—List 4

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

EDNT EDNT = Exegetical Dictionary of the NT—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 97
